seo.title: Tool Loop Detection in AI Agents in OpenAI Assistants API Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix tool loop detection in ai agents in OpenAI Assistants API agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In OpenAI Assistants API, Tool Loop Detection identifies when agents enter infinite cycles of redundant tool calls, such as repeatedly invoking the same function or code interpreter without progressing toward task completion. This manifests as excessive run steps, ballooning thread context, and stalled assistant responses due to the API's stateful run loop continuing until max iterations or timeouts. Common triggers include ambiguous function schemas leading to repetitive calls and code interpreter loops generating infinite outputs.

howItManifests.scenario: Code interpreter repeatedly executes the same analysis
howItManifests.symptom: Run status shows 20+ 'requires_action' steps with identical code interpreter tool calls
howItManifests.impact: Hits OpenAI run limit (100 steps), thread context exceeds 128k tokens, $10+ per failed run

howItManifests.scenario: File search tool called in tight loop on same directory
howItManifests.symptom: Sequential 'file_search' tool calls with overlapping vector store queries
howItManifests.impact: Irrelevant document retrieval cascades, degrading response relevance by 80%

howItManifests.scenario: Custom function ping-pongs between two interdependent calls
howItManifests.impact: Assistant stuck in loop, user experiences 5+ minute hangs

howItManifests.scenario: Thread context accumulation from unterminated tool chains
howItManifests.symptom: Run metadata shows step count >50 with repeating tool_ids
howItManifests.impact: Silent failure as context hits model limits, incomplete responses

detection.name: Run Steps Loop Analysis
detection.description: Parse run events to detect >10 identical tool calls within 20 steps

detection.name: Function Call Cycle Detection
detection.description: Track function call graph to identify cycles using networkx

detection.name: Realtime Run Monitoring
detection.description: TypeScript webhook handler for run.step events detecting loops

fixes.name: Max Tool Calls Per Run
fixes.description: Configure assistant with tool call limits to prevent runaway loops

fixes.name: Loop-Breaking System Instructions
fixes.description: Instruct assistant to detect and break loops explicitly
fixes.code:
assistant = client.beta.assistants.update(
    assistant_id,
    instructions="""CRITICAL: Detect tool loops and STOP.
    If you've called the same tool 3+ times without progress:
    1. Summarize findings
    2. Respond directly to user
    3. Do NOT call tools again
    Track your tool calls:"""
)

fixes.name: Run Termination Guardrails
fixes.description: TypeScript interceptor to cancel looping runs

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Tool Loop Detection in AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates as a webhook on OpenAI run completion events, automatically analyzing run steps for tool call patterns and detecting loops before they exhaust quotas. Using Vex's pattern recognition, it identifies repeating tool_ids, function call cycles, and context bloat in real-time across all your assistants. When loops are detected, Vex can auto-remediate by canceling runs, updating assistant instructions, or triggering fallback human review.

The Vex SDK provides pre-built detectors for OpenAI-specific failure modes including tool loops, with one-line integration into your existing run polling loops. Vex maintains historical baselines per assistant, alerting only on statistically significant loop behaviors while ignoring legitimate iterative tool use. Remediation workflows auto-generate loop-breaking instructions and deploy updated assistant configs.

Vex dashboards provide loop heatmaps by tool type, assistant, and thread, enabling proactive schema improvements before loops impact users.

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Install Vex SDK now and eliminate tool loops across all your OpenAI assistants in 5 minutes
